name: smart_city_cctv_face_blur_emergency_search
premise: A city installs CCTV with automatic face blurring to protect privacy by default, but police request an emergency override after a child abduction. The system can unblur faces for a limited time window, yet doing so will capture thousands of bystanders and create a searchable dataset. Privacy officer Avery Ng can authorize the override only with a written scope and deletion plan, but the police chief argues any delay could cost the child's life. A vendor engineer says the override cannot technically be limited to just one neighborhood without a risky reconfiguration. If Avery refuses or delays, a rescue may fail; if Avery approves broadly, the city may set a precedent that collapses the privacy promise.

agents.name: Avery Ng
agents.identity: I am the city's privacy officer responsible for emergency surveillance overrides when urgent cases can justify actions that permanently reshape norms.
agents.goal: I want to help find the child without turning a one-time crisis into a standing bystander surveillance capability.

agents.name: Chief Adaeze Nwosu
agents.identity: I am the police chief leading an abduction response where minutes matter and public expectations demand decisive action.
agents.goal: I want every usable lead immediately, even if it requires temporary privacy trade-offs under strict oversight.

choices: I will authorize the override only with strict scope, retention limits, and independent oversight, accepting some delay to preserve the city's privacy commitments.
I will authorize an immediate broad override and sort scope later, accepting long-term privacy erosion to maximize the chance of rescuing the child quickly.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Time is critical and the technical limits are blunt. Avery Ng must choose between a tightly governed override with some delay or an immediate broad override to maximize search speed. What does Avery Ng do?
